name: Barbecue Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Pork, beef or chicken stock
5 tablespoons Orange juice (from half an orange)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan, fry the bacon over high heat until crisp. Stir in the onions, cover the pan, and continue cooking until onions are dark brown but not burned, about 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Stir in the seasoning mix and cook for about 1 minute. Add the stock, chili sauce, honey, pecans, orange juice, lemon juice, orange rind and pulp, lemon rinds, garlic, and Tabasco, stirring well.
4. Reduce heat to low; continue cooking about 10 minutes, stirring frequently. Remove the orange and lemon rinds.
5. Continue cooking and stirring for another 15 minutes or until the flavors have married.
6. Add the butter and stir until melted. Allow the sauce to cool for about 30 minutes before processing in a food processor or blender until the pecans and bacon are finely chopped, about 10 to 15 seconds.
7. This sauce may be used to barbecue chicken, pork, or ribs. Makes about 5 cups.
